html:
Ice is a Food item in the Reign of Giants DLC, obtained by mining Mini Glaciers. It can be used to craft various temperature-reducing items, added to an Ice Cube to repair it, eaten to reduce the player's temperature by 40 degrees for 7.5 seconds, or used to extinguish Fire and smoldering objects. Ice is also used in a few Crock Pot recipes, or can be used as a filler, much like Twigs.

Ice melts in three days. It will never melt when put in an Ice Box or when the world temperature is at or below 0 degrees (typically only in Winter) and will melt much faster when being near a fire (at a rate of 30). If the player is still holding a chunk of Ice while it melts, Wetness will be increased by 2 points per unit of ice.

Downloadable Content

Ice is also included in the Shipwrecked DLC, as well as the craftables it is used for. Mini Glaciers are not found here, however, Ice can be obtained by refining Hail that can be collected in Hurricane Season. The DLC also introduces the Ice Maker 3000, which can produce Ice. It is also used in several more Crock Pot recipes, including Bisque and Ceviche.

In the Hamlet DLC, Ice can be bought for 1 Oinc at Swinesbury Fine Grocer's and is required to craft Sprinklers.

Recipes

Filler can't be Meats, Vegetables, Eggs, or Twigs
Filler can't be Meats or Vegetables
Fillers cannot be Meats or Fishes.
Using Wobster will produce Wobster Bisque instead.

×2

×1.5

Tips

  • Ice can be used as an edible filler ingredient in most Crock Pot recipes, greatly increasing the bonus points of those recipes. Because Ice never spoils in an Ice Box, it is recommended to keep several stacks of Ice in an Ice Box next to Crock Pots.
  • It's recommended to eat some Ice if the player needs to lower their temperature during Summer or Dry Season.

Trivia

  • When Ice melts, it gains the prefixes of "Melting" and "Melted" instead of "Stale" and "Spoiled", like other perishable foods.

Gallery

  • The "Extinguish" option when holding Ice over the Campfire.
  • Old variant of Ice

Ice is a Food item in the Reign of Giants DLC, obtained by mining Mini Glaciers. It can be used to craft various temperature-reducing items, added to an Ice Cube to repair it, eaten to reduce the player's temperature by 40 degrees for 7.5 seconds, or used to extinguish Fire and smoldering objects. Ice is also used in a few Crock Pot recipes, or can be used as a filler, much like Twigs.
Ice melts in three days. It will never melt when put in an Ice Box or when the world temperature is at or below 0 degrees (typically only in Winter) and will melt much faster when being near a fire (at a rate of 30). If the player is still holding a chunk of Ice while it melts, Wetness will be increased by 2 points per unit of ice.
Downloadable Content[edit | edit source]
Ice is also included in the Shipwrecked DLC, as well as the craftables it is used for. Mini Glaciers are not found here, however, Ice can be obtained by refining Hail that can be collected in Hurricane Season. The DLC also introduces the Ice Maker 3000, which can produce Ice. It is also used in several more Crock Pot recipes, including Bisque and Ceviche.
In the Hamlet DLC, Ice can be bought for 1 Oinc at Swinesbury Fine Grocer's and is required to craft Sprinklers.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Nước Đá là một Thực Phẩm trong DLC ​​Reign of Giants, thu được bằng cách khai thác Sông Băng Nhỏ. Nó có thể được sử dụng để chế tạo nhiều vật phẩm giảm nhiệt độ khác nhau, thêm vào Khối băng để sửa chữa, ăn để giảm nhiệt độ của người chơi xuống 40 độ trong 7,5 giây hoặc dùng để dập tắt Lửa và các vật thể đang cháy âm ỉ. Đá cũng được sử dụng trong một số công thức nấu Nồi Hầm, hoặc có thể dùng làm chất độn, giống như Cành Cây.

'''Ice''' is a [[Food]] [[item]] in the ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, obtained by mining [[Mini Glacier]]s. It can be used to craft various temperature-reducing items, added to an [[Ice Cube]] to repair it, eaten to reduce the player's temperature by 40 degrees for 7.5 seconds, or used to extinguish [[Fire]] and smoldering objects. Ice is also used in a few [[Crock Pot]] recipes, or can be used as a filler, much like [[Twigs]].

Ice [[Durability|melts]] in three days. It will never melt when put in an [[Ice Box]] or when the world temperature is at or below 0 degrees (typically only in [[Seasons/Winter|Winter]]) and will melt much faster when being near a fire (at a rate of 30). If the player is still holding a chunk of Ice while it melts, [[Wetness]] will be increased by 2 points per unit of ice.

=={{pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content ==
Ice is also included in the ''[[Shipwrecked]]'' DLC, as well as the craftables it is used for. Mini Glaciers are not found here, however, Ice can be obtained by [[Refine|refining]] [[Hail]] that can be collected in [[Hurricane Season]]. The DLC also introduces the [[Ice Maker 3000]], which can produce Ice. It is also used in several more Crock Pot recipes, including [[Bisque]] and [[Ceviche]].

In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Ice can be bought for 1 [[Oinc]] at [[Swinesbury Fine Grocer's]] and is required to craft [[Sprinkler]]s.

== {{Pic32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* Ice can be used as an edible filler ingredient in most [[Crock Pot]] recipes, greatly increasing the bonus points of those recipes. Because Ice never spoils in an [[Ice Box]], it is recommended to keep several stacks of Ice in an [[Ice Box]] next to Crock Pots.
*It's recommended to eat some Ice if the player needs to lower their temperature during [[Seasons/Summer|Summer]] or [[Seasons/Dry|Dry Season]].

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* When Ice melts, it gains the prefixes of "Melting" and "Melted" instead of "Stale" and "Spoiled", like other perishable foods.
